pose: The astromech's wheels whir quietly, then freeze, still focused on Starlight. "So, this little number's idea of 'feminine curves'..." A dry, mirthless buzz. "I've seen more flattering curves on a Hutt. And yet she has that... presence." A cold electronic hiss.

pose: The droid's dome tilts, his holocam eye narrowing as he speaks. "Oh, I'm just delighted to be surrounded by the great and the good while I'm still stuck with this bland, pre-factory-issue paint job. I swear, if she gets her hands on me one more time... FINE. A low, acidic zot. "

scene: "Fascinating," that's the only word that Lynae can come up with. What a roiling boil of rage Zubindi's words manage to evoke. Rage and fascination but mostly ra
pose:
The astromech droid's gaze fixes on Zubindi, outrage sparking across his face like sparks from an electrum taser. "FUCKING HONOR THE DEAD, ZUBINDI." Zubindi pauses for dramatic effect once more, leaving Cricket no room to argue, before he continues speaking. Cricket seethes through Zubindi's introduction, offended by the notion of Basca Starker's corpse being used for public entertainment, his dome oscillating in disgust as if there were enough slack to allow him to do it. He thinks this all very fittingly tasteless.%r
"RAGE IN ACTION!" The astromech droid yells out, red-lit eye glowing malevolently.

context: scene: Starlight may seem cold and remote, at first glance, but in truth it is memory. The memory of light and of distant worlds, of places and people and history. The
scene: The music softens signaling the start of some sort of announcement.
pose:
@emit
The little astromech's dome snaps back towards Sexy Red Guard, panel flashing crimson. "A pirate, huh? Great. Just what we need - a swashbuckling relic of the Old Republic to complicate things." Cricket clicks and whirs disdainfully. "What's next? Mandalorians, Jedi, and a certain Sith Lord who thinks he can outmaneuver Johanna?" He pivots, presenting an unobstructed view of his rear compartment emblazoned with Imperial crest. "At least I don't hide behind some pathetic facade. What are you really, huh? A glorified smuggler with delusions of grandeur?"

context: scene: Starlight may seem cold and remote, at first glance, but in truth it is memory. The memory of light and of distant worlds, of places and people and history. The
scene: The music softens signaling the start of some sort of announcement.
pose: The astromech's eyes flicker from Starlight to Veruca, weighing their presence before spinning back to face the petite guard. "Heh, cute uniform. Reminds me of something I used to wear." A low chuckle rumbles through Cricket's frame. "A sexy Red Guard, huh? That title's got a whole different meaning when attached to a piece of ass like her." His whirring laughter drips with sarcasm. "Bet she can't even shoot worth a damn, but look who's talking - an old rust bucket like me." His dome swivels, scanning the crowd once more before zeroing in on Kasha. "And what's a party without a Woodcarving Feline, right? Always good to see the charity set showing up to gawk at the real players." He lets out a dry whistle. "I wonder if that pirate chick over there knows how ridiculous she looks?" Cricket muses, nodding towards Veruca's elaborate costume. "Probably thinks it makes her edgy and cool. Please. It"

pose:
Cricket's holocam flickers, processing this new revelation. "A new taser? Oh, YOU BETCHA he needs a new taser," the R2 says sarcastically, his wheels spinning up to speed. "This old one was just FINE. Not like I've been begging for a kill setting all these years..." 

He cuts himself off with an electronic huff, realizing that wasn't entirely fair of him to say around Atsvara. Still, he can't help but be a bit miffed - ak always does make such a production out of everything. The droid engineer was probably still savoring his momentary fame as Sir Doctor ak after Cricket's last "audition" on Cloud City. Cricket snorts derisively at the memory.

"Well, don't just stand there, ak! Get cracking!" Cricket demands impatiently, tapping a wheel in agitation. "And this time, let's not skimp on the amps, huh? Maybe go for something... REALLY special." His tone takes on a sly edge and he winks

pose:
@emit
The astromech's electronic whine takes on a sullen note as he spins towards Atsvara, his chrome-plated underbelly gleaming ominously in the dim light of the Auxiliary Command Suite. "You think this is about me needing a new taser? Ha! I've had a custom electrum one since before you were in diapers, kid." Cricket's camera flickers, zeroing in on Ak's AC Sphere with a predatory glint.

"Besides, I was just starting to enjoy making Johanna squirm over her lack of foresight. You're not helping my cause here!" He pauses to let that sink in before continuing, a sneer creeping into his binary chirps. "I require a weapon capable of delivering 500,000 volts without overheating after three seconds, and I need it yesterday."

Cricket's pincer arm extends, curling into a mocking approximation of a trigger finger. "And another thing: what makes you think ak would help us? He's too busy polishing his own genius to waste time on lowly"

pose: @emit The astromech whirls to face Lynae, panel flashing red. "Oh, I get it now," he drawls, tone dripping with disdain. "You're into that whole 'beauty and the beast' thing, huh? Well, good luck finding beauty in a corpse. Basca Starker was nothing but a monster, even when he had a human face." A pause, just long enough for Cricket to adjust his festive attire before continuing. "But hey, who am I to judge? You seem to have found something to spark your interest. Just don't come crying to me when you realize rage isn't warm and fuzzy after all. It'll burn you down like everything else in this galaxy."

context: Zubindi Hakoon: Zubindi pauses for a few moments to allow the full perverse concept to be absorbed the his audience. "So we present to you Grotesques and Odalisques, a requiem
scene: "Fascinating," that's the only word that Lynae can come up with. What a roiling boil of rage Zubindi's words manage to evoke. Rage and fascination but mostly ra
pose:
@emit
The astromech droid's gaze locks onto Lynae's as she contemplates Basca's corpse and rages against the injustice of it all. "Oh, I'm sure he did love her," Cricket remarks dryly, "In between developing bioweapons and Sith-infesting himself, no doubt." His dome tilts to regard Zubindi thoughtfully for a moment before the little droid continues, "Fascinating, indeed. It's almost poetic how well he embodies tonight's theme. The man who brought us both beauty and beastliness." Cricket wheels closer to where Lynae stands, eyeing her drink speculatively. "You know, I once had a master who tried to drill 'propriety' into me. Can you imagine? As if an astromech could ever be polite enough not to electrocute his owner when provoked. Ha!" He snorts, a short, derisive sound. "I'd show that fat cow what propriety looks like: zapping her right on her pompous posterior the next time she"

pose: "Sexy Red Guard%rThis human woman is fairly petite at only 5'4", with slim hips and slender shoulders with feminine curves. Constant training throughout her life has made her slight frame strong. Her manner and walk are often at odds - the sometimes cocky swagger of a swoop racer or the stiff residue of old military training, both often juxtaposed to a practiced and well engrained grace of a stateswoman when her attire or the occasion call for it. Her jet black hair is shoulder length, cut in layers that frame her face, and streaked with emerging white strands. She wears tasteful make-up, with soft pink lips, smouldering eyes, and nails neatly manicured and polished.%rUnder delicately arched brows, ice-blue eyes exhibit a tell-tale spark of intelligence. Her pleasantly attractive face has a strong chin. Freckles are lightly sprinkled across the bridge of a rather cute nose, which wrinkles if she allows herself to smile. pose:
%r%r%t The little droid's dome spins back to Atsvara, eyes flashing red, as he scrambles to recover his dignity. "Rots their brains? Makes parents upset?" he scoffs. "More like makes them jealous of my superior collection. I'm an ELDER STATES-DROID, Missy! I've seen more than your parents ever WILL." He wheels closer to her, lowering his voice to a conspiratorial whirr. "Besides, that stuff's not for me. It's for my fans. They love it. I get requests all the time. It's ART."

%r%r%t Sir ak's arrival breaks the tension, and Cricket pivots toward him eagerly. "SIR AK!" he squeals, practically buzzing with glee. "Oh, I am so glad you're here, Sir ak! Yes, yes, I need a new taser, the most magnificent electrum one you can dream up. With spikes. And a kill setting. I've been TELLING Ats"

context: scene: Quickly desiring to change the topic and get things back on track, she looks to Vee-Won and simply asks, "Is he here?" Vee-Won's response is simply to shout bac

pose:
The droid's dome spins frantically, a chaotic whirr of embarrassment and outrage. "Oh, it rots their brains, does it?!" Cricket bleats. "How DARE you tell me how to raise my own droidling, you pint-sized imp! I'll show you what happens when you sass your elders!" He lunges forward, saw-arm whirring, before catching himself. No... no, tasing the young Baroness on her own flagship would be a mistake. Even for him.

"Y-You know what?" he stammers, trying to regain his composure. "Maybe... maybe we should talk about your holo collection instead, miss high-and-mighty. What would Sir Doctor ak think about his favorite young prodigy sneaking around in the archives? Huh?! I bet he'd have a LOT to say about that!"

He turns to the towering Xi Charrian, eyes wide and pleading. "Sir ak! Please, tell this insolent brat that I'm not the one who needs a lecture here"

pose:
Cricket's dome whirls, holocam eye flicking between the gruesome centerpiece and Lynae's expression, taking in her fury. He can relate; he hates Basca too. "Rage is a warm fuzzy, indeed! I've been there." The droid zots derisively. "Maybe we should tase the bastard, for old times' sake? He'd look good crispy."

He wheels closer to the doctor, hissing conspiratorially, "Johanna once said she should have just shot him, spawn or no. She was right. They all were, the lot of them." A quick glance around -- Johanna isn't here, but Atsvara is, chatting with some other guests. The droid lowers his voice further. "Between you and me, I think our beloved Baroness would love to see this fucker roast. What do you say, doc?"

The droid's dome tilts, considering. "No? Okay, fair enough. But if you change your mind..." He extends a pincer arm

context: Zubindi Hakoon: Zubindi pauses for a few moments to allow the full perverse concept to be absorbed the his audience. "So we present to you Grotesques and Odalisques, a requiem
scene: "Fascinating," that's the only word that Lynae can come up with. What a roiling boil of rage Zubindi's words manage to evoke. Rage and fascination but mostly ra
pose:
%rThe droid's dome spins toward Lynae, panels flashing red. "Fascinating? Really? I suppose it would be to a heartless sack of bolts like YOU. What's that word you meatbags use? Ah, right! Empathy. Never heard of it."
%r"Crime against nature," Cricket scoffs at the corpse's display, wheels spinning as he surveys the grotesque scene. "I mean, look at him. Twice the monster, half the man. No wonder the cow got rid of him." He snorts derisively. "He should've known better than to fuck with Caspia. Now he gets to rot while we get to party. Win-win!"
